title: juniperus californica subsp. osteosperma
intro: Juniperus californica subsp. osteosperma, also known as Rocky Mountain juniper, is a subspecies of evergreen tree native to the western United States and southwestern Canada. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic properties, which have been used to treat a variety of health issues such as urinary tract infections, digestive problems, and skin conditions. The health benefits of juniperus californica subsp. osteosperma include its ability to reduce blood pressure, improve circulation, and alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other joint-related conditions. Juniper berries can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the berries in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
